---
title: "Hyland Credentials For Legal Hold"
---

Hyland Credentials for legal holds are digital hashes which verify that the content put on legal hold is not altered or tampered. A regulator or a legal party can be assured that the legal hold was correctly placed, maintained, and revoked through a cryptography technology.

When a legal hold is requested, the record is verified through cryptographic hashing to provide stronger proof of compliance with legal hold requirements. Hyland Credentials API is used to generate a verifiable credential at the time that a record is added to a legal hold.

You can see the following icons on the records list in the Records Management section of Governance:

| Icon | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | Displayed for records that are not on hold. |
|  | Displayed for records that are pending a credential hash. |
|  | Displayed for records that have the credential hash. |

![A page icon displayed](data:image/png;base64,)    ![A shield with a clock icon displayed](data:image/png;base64,)    ![A shield with a padlock icon displayed](data:image/png;base64,)
